commutecompute.name: CommuteCompute Engine
commutecompute.description: Multi-state GTFS-RT support (VIC/NSW/QLD live, SA/WA/TAS/NT/ACT timetable fallback), coordinate-based stop detection (226 VIC metro + 1637 tram + 4151 bus + expanded fallback stops for all states), state-aware suburb-to-stop mappings for all capital cities, graceful no-GTFS-RT degradation, runtime line verification, Metro Tunnel citybound detection (VIC), direction-based train filtering, route-aware transit filtering, Departure Confidence, Sleep Optimiser, Alt Transit, Lifestyle Context, Mindset analysis

renderer.name: CCDash Renderer
renderer.description: V16.0 spec-compliant rendering with per-leg element scaling (prevents overlap with 7+ legs), coffee leg redesign (no number bubble, no time box, coffee icon replaces [OK], centre-aligned text), route/line names in transit titles, transit countdown fix, cafe closed case-insensitive matching, confidence context line rendering, subtitle overflow protection, lifestyle obligation styling, suburb locations, unified cafe closed/skipped rendering, diversion status bar

adminPanel.description: Admin Panel unified with single /api/commutecompute endpoint (no separate screen.js), KV-persisting address edits, geocoding, Places API autocomplete, dynamic version loading, alt transit data, TRMNL-free branding

setupWizard.description: Initial Setup Wizard with BLE Provisioning

livedash.description: CC LiveDash™ Multi-Device Endpoint — deduplicated, imports shared config-token decoder

server.description: Unified Vercel Serverless API — single /api/commutecompute endpoint serves JSON, PNG, and BMP. /api/screen backward-compatible via rewrite.

firmware.description: ESP32-C3 CCFirm - BLE Provisioning + BMP Text Renderer + Battery Optimisation

rules.MAJOR: Incompatible API changes, major feature overhauls

changes: Fix headway projection phantom departures: mark projected departures as timetable estimates (isTimetableEstimate: true) — shows 'Scheduled ~Xmin' not false LIVE precision. Increased default headway from 8 to 12 min (trams) and 10 to 15 min (trains) for more realistic late-night estimates.

Fix tram route variant matching in subtitle padding: use numeric prefix comparison (parseInt) so route variants (e.g., 58a) match the base route (58) — previously strict string comparison excluded variant departures from 'Next:' subtitle.

Fix tram route: journey-context filtering. At intersections, filter detected tram routes to those that ALSO serve the destination station area (1km radius). A route that only passes through the home intersection but doesn't go near the station is eliminated. Resolves the route flipping issue after 8 prior fix attempts (nearest-stop, frequency, Redis preference all failed).
Fix disruption matching: text-based line name fallback now runs even when affectedRoutes is present but format doesn't match line code patterns. Previously fell through to strict rejection.
Fix sleep display: when bedtime has passed, secondary line now shows bedtime context instead of duplicating alarm time.

Fix tram route stability at intersections: frequency-based selection from all detected routes near user coordinates. Nearest-stop approach was unreliable (route flipped every refresh due to sub-metre distance differences). Now counts live departures per detected route — the route with most departures is the primary service, stable across refreshes.

Fix disruption matching: add text-based line name fallback when GTFS-RT alerts have no explicit affectedRoutes. Alerts mentioning the line name in title/description now match the leg. Resolves long-term timetable change alerts not showing on dashboard.
Fix tram route detection: populate tramRouteNumber from coordinate-based nearest-stop detection (was always null). Use as highest priority in route selection — overrides stale Redis preferences.

Fix tram timing: only apply GTFS-RT delay offset for trains (scheduled time convention). Tram/bus feeds use predicted times (delay already included) — adding delay double-counted, inflating departure times.

Fix tram route selection: engine route (from nearby stops detection) takes priority over stale Redis preference — v5.5.8 saved wrong route (86 instead of 58) which v5.5.11 locked to. Engine now overrides stale preference when it has live data.
Fix route_number property mismatch: engine used snake_case but findNearestStops returns camelCase. Now checks both forms for Redis backward compatibility.

Fix delayed train timing: GTFS-RT departure.time is scheduled time — add delay offset to get actual predicted departure. Delayed trains (e.g. +22 min) were shown at their scheduled time, causing catchability filter to reject them as already departed when they were still at the station.

Fix disruption-to-leg mapping: severe mode-wide alerts (SIGNIFICANT_DELAYS, NO_SERVICE, REDUCED_SERVICE) without explicit route IDs now match all legs of that mode — previously silently dropped because affectedRoutes was empty.

Fix disruption leg state: matched disruptions now set leg state to delayed/suspended with parsed delay minutes from alert text (e.g. 'up to 15 minutes' -> +15 MIN) — enables renderer to show delay info on affected legs.

Fix City Loop empty-result fallback: when no trains have confirmed passesCityLoop (GTFS-RT feed only includes upcoming stops, not full trip), treat non-Metro-Tunnel citybound trains as likely City Loop — was returning empty array causing fallback to distant trains at 55+ min instead of nearby trains at 7-18 min

Fix tram route stability: trust preferredTramRoute when it has live departures in the feed — stops route flipping between 58/72/16 at intersections where most-frequent-route was unstable between refreshes. Only auto-detect when no preference set.
Fix disruption visibility: add MINOR_DELAY and OTHER_EFFECT to MATERIAL_EFFECTS filter — minor delays (up to 15 min) and cancelled services were silently dropped and not shown on dashboard

Fix RENDER_ERROR: remove undefined cafeIsBusy/cafeIsQuiet variables — v5.5.8 introduced references to non-existent variables, causing ReferenceError on every render. Use coffeeDecision?.busyLevel instead.
Fix RENDER_ERROR after preferences reset: add optional chaining to coffeeDecision?.canGet — null coffeeDecision (no route data after reset) crashed the entire render pipeline
Fix stale preferredTramRoute priority: live detection now runs first, saved preference is fallback only. Auto-save overwrites stale routes when live detection finds a different route.
Fix departure count: supplement from same-direction departures when route filter reduces matchedDepartures below 3 — ensures 3 departure times in Next: subtitle
Fix coffee timing display: add cafe_wait_time and cafe_busyness to API response. Renderer shows TIME FOR COFFEE in busyness-only path when API reports coffee is available.
Fix auto-save overwrite: liveRoute !== preferredTramRoute triggers Redis update (previously only saved when null)

Fix tram coord-proximity silent rejection: remove route-number guard that discarded all trip matches when no feed stop IDs had coordinates in static reference table. The 600m search radius already constrains matching — route guard was causing complete tram cascade failure, showing Scheduled when live data existed in the feed.
Auto-save preferredTramRoute to Redis on first detection from live feed — ensures correct route available on cold starts and subsequent requests without manual configuration

Restore empty-feed-cache-poisoning guard: only cache GTFS-RT feeds with entity count > 0 — empty feeds from transient API responses were poisoning the cache for 75 seconds, causing scheduled fallback on all subsequent requests until cache expired

Fix tram route misidentification: guard parseInt(String(null))=NaN fallback — when leg has no route, pick most-frequent route from live departures instead of falling back to ALL routes (which picks wrong route at intersections)

Fix train City Loop filtering: include ALL City Loop trains plus nearby direct trains — previously excluded valid alternating line services, showing wrong departure times (40, 77 min instead of NOW, 13, 15)
Fix train departure padding: skip route number filter for trains (use direction + City Loop/Tunnel filtering instead) — ensures 3 departure times from interleaved lines
Fix tram departure padding: exclude Metro Tunnel trains from City Loop padding to prevent wrong-line leakage
Fix findNearestStops multi-route: collect nearest stop per route at intersections — prevents wrong route when one route's stop is 1m closer
Fix tram route extraction timing: pick most-frequent route from live departures before building journey legs — ensures correct route identification on first render
Add live departure verification rule to DEVELOPMENT-RULES.md Section 23.7
Add state-gating rule to DEVELOPMENT-RULES.md Section 24.1
Add privacy scope extension to DEVELOPMENT-RULES.md Section 1.1 (plans, agent prompts, memory)
cc-dev-validate.sh v2.2: 3 new checks (state-gating, verification rule, multi-state rule)

Fix coffee decision disruption detection: calculateCoffeeDecision received Array instead of String — isDisrupted() always returned false via API path. Now constructs alert text from disruption headerText fields.
Expand MATERIAL_EFFECTS filter: add UNKNOWN_EFFECT and STOP_CLOSURE — real disruptions with GTFS default effect classification were silently dropped
Complete disruption line code map: add missing metro lines, add bus route number matching — buses and minor metro lines now receive disruption alerts
Reduce GTFS-RT feed cache TTL from 120s to 75s — halves worst-case departure staleness while respecting API rate limits
Reduce BMP Cache-Control max-age from 20s to 15s — combined with feed cache reduction, worst-case staleness drops from 2:20 to 1:30

Fix premature timetable marking: filterUnavailableTransitLegs now keeps all departures when route-specific filter returns empty but mode feed has live data — consistent with train path fallback behaviour
Fix dataSource ternary: add 'timetable' state when legs have isTimetableEstimate but API key present — previously showed 'no-data' when timetable estimates were displayed
Fix departure padding route filter: pad from raw feed filtered by route number for trams/buses — prevents wrong-route departures polluting Next: subtitle
Widen renderer departure filter from 60 to 90 minutes — supports multi-leg journeys where later stops are 50-70 min away
Add ferry to hasAnyLiveData: ferry live data now contributes to LIVE badge, isPartialLive, and dataSource detection
Coffee decision isDisrupted: add type guard, expand keywords (no service, significant delays, modified service, stop closure), case-insensitive matching

Fix City Loop passesCityLoop default: change from !isMetroTunnel to false when no trip scan and no finalStop data — prevents incorrectly classifying direct-to-Flinders evening services as City Loop trains. Conservative default lets findMatchingDeparture fallback show all citybound services.

City Loop time-gated soft-preference: when nearest City Loop train is >30 min later than nearest direct train, include all non-Metro-Tunnel citybound services — prevents 80+ min waits when evening services bypass the City Loop. User can alight at Flinders Street and walk to City Loop stations.

Tram coord-proximity fallback coordinates: use tram stop's actual coordinates from VIC_TRAM_STOPS_WITH_COORDS instead of home coordinates — centres the 300m search at the boarding stop, not the home address (which may be 500m+ away).
Widen coord-proximity radius to 600m when using fallback coordinates (stop not in database) — compensates for home-to-stop distance offset when stop coordinates unavailable.

Merge tram cascade tiers: when coord-proximity finds < 3 departures, also run all-trips-scan and merge results — catches trips that coord-proximity missed due to unmatchable feed stop IDs, providing 3+ departures for Next: display
Extend raw feed departure padding to all transit modes (tram, bus) — previously only trains/vline. Richer Next: frequency context across all modes.

Strict City Loop filtering: remove soft-preference fallback that accepted non-City-Loop trains when City Loop services were > 20 min away. Trains that do not serve the alighting station must never be shown regardless of frequency gap.

GTFS-RT feed cache with 2-minute TTL: cache successful API responses in-memory, return stale cache on transient API failures — prevents live/scheduled alternation between refreshes. Departure countdowns recalculated dynamically from cached absolute timestamps.

Remove closest-route filter from coord-proximity: return departures from ALL routes at intersection stops — provides richer Next: display with departures across multiple serving routes instead of single-route restriction
Extract tram route name from live departures: when route engine has no route number, extract from GTFS-RT departure data to prevent title flipping between named route and generic mode label

Coord-proximity flexible ID matching: build pre-computed lookup map with trailing-numeric extraction — resolves GTFS-RT prefixed stop IDs (e.g. aus:vic:tram-1567) never matching static bare-numeric IDs (1567). Root cause of tram live data never flowing through v5.4.5-v5.4.8

City Loop trip-scan: add passesCityLoop flag by scanning full stop sequence for City Loop platform IDs — replaces finalStop-only check which failed when citybound trains terminate past City Loop (outbound terminus). Prevents wrong line selection for City Loop destinations

Revert auto-detected tram route from nearest stop name: at intersections the nearest stop may be from a different route (e.g. Route 129) than what the user rides (Route 58) — only route engine and user preference are reliable sources

Widen no-route-filter coord-proximity radius from 150m to 300m: 150m was too narrow to find tram stops at intersections, 300m gives enough reach while closest-route logic disambiguates multiple routes
Revert detectStopIdsFromAddress tramRouteNumber to null: keep route extraction in findNearestStops for logging only

Tram route number extraction: extract route number from VIC_TRAM_STOPS_WITH_COORDS stop names (#NN suffix) in findNearestStops and return via detectStopIdsFromAddress — enables coord-proximity 500m radius with route filter instead of 150m without
Use preferredTramRoute in tramApiOptions: add user-configured preferred tram route as fallback source for coord-proximity route filtering (priority: route engine > preference > auto-detected)
Fix renderer subtitle duplication: add Scheduled startsWith check to renderer gate that appends Next: departures — prevents Scheduled ~X min and ~Next: X min showing simultaneously

Train departure padding from raw feed: merge same-direction raw feed departures into nextDepartureTimesMs when City Loop or route filtering reduces matched departures — provides richer Next: frequency context

Confidence-gated tram direction filter: require >= 3 coord stops and >= 500m geographic span before applying direction rejection — prevents false rejection when GTFS-RT/static stop ID namespace mismatch leaves sparse coordCache

Broad fallback stopId-aware matching: pass target stopId to processAnyRouteDepartures so it matches the user's actual station before falling back to earliest-future heuristic — fixes wrong departure times from mid-route stops

Timetable fallback always generates headway-based departure estimates: removes feedHadEntities guard so Scheduled subtitle shows departure countdown (8, 16, 24 min) instead of transit duration (~6min) when GTFS-RT entities exist but filtering removed all matches
Contact email separation: info@commutecompute.com for general/security/privacy enquiries, licensing@commutecompute.com for licensing only — SECURITY.md, PRIVACY.md, privacy.html, admin.html updated

Tram direction filter v2: use trip endpoint coordinates (first/last stops with coords in coordCache) to filter wrong-direction trams — more robust than v5.4.0 single-lookahead which failed on unmapped stops
Remove redundant Alight at subtitle: leg title already shows 'to [destination]' which implies the alighting stop — saves e-ink display space
Destination coordinate passthrough restored for coord-proximity direction filtering

Fix Next: departure subtitle suppression: clear stale isTimetableEstimate flag when findMatchingDeparture returns live GTFS-RT data — resolves conflict between filterUnavailableTransitLegs strict route matching and findMatchingDeparture any-route fallback
Fix renderer Next: rebuild on empty subtitle: allow departure time recomputation when API subtitle is empty (previously required non-empty subtitle to run rebuild block)
Update contact email: licensing@commutecompute.com for licensing enquiries, info@commutecompute.com for general contact — 13 files updated across documentation, legal, and HTML pages

Restore Next: departure padding for service frequency display — when fewer than 3 catchable departures exist, pad with future-from-now departures up to 3. The DEPART time box shows the actual catchable departure; Next: provides service frequency context

Fix City Loop destination label: when soft-preference accepts a non-City-Loop train, use GTFS-RT headsign (actual terminus) instead of hardcoded 'City' for destination — prevents impossible route labels like a direct-to-Flinders line shown as going to a City Loop station
Revert tram direction filter: GTFS-RT stop ID namespace mismatch made lookahead coordinates unavailable, causing coord-proximity to return no results and trams to fall back to timetable
Revert cascade < 3 threshold: mixed-tier results caused wrong routes (Route 78 instead of 58) when route-level merged with coord-proximity
Revert City Loop destination label override: liveData.destination is always 'City' for citybound trains, incorrectly overriding all train destinations including Frankston-through-Loop services
Fix renderer Next: stripping: only strip and recompute when nextDepartureTimesMs is available — preserves API subtitle for timetable fallback legs

Tram direction filtering: coord-proximity now filters wrong-direction trams using stop-sequence lookahead relative to destination coordinates — prevents matching opposite-direction services at shared stops
Force 3 live catchable: tram cascade continues to lower tiers when fewer than 3 departures found (with tripId dedup) — ensures 3 live departure times when service frequency supports it
Catchable-only Next display: removed uncatchable departure padding from Next: subtitle — only departures the user can physically reach are shown
Renderer catchability recomputation: renderer always recomputes Next: from absolute timestamps instead of trusting API subtitle — belt-and-suspenders against uncatchable leakage

City Loop label accuracy: when soft-preference accepts a non-City-Loop train (late evening), destination label updated to the train's actual terminus instead of showing an unreachable City Loop station
Tram destination clarity: tram alight destinations now show abbreviated station suffix (Stn) to distinguish station area from suburb name
Fix tram route identification: findMatchingDeparture now prefers configured route over any-route fallback — prevents wrong route from nearby intersections overriding the correct one
Fix train departure timing: enabled all-trips-scan for metro mode with direction detection and Metro Tunnel filtering — produces accurate stop-level times instead of broad fallback median heuristic
Fix broad fallback heuristic: use earliest future departure instead of median — median picked mid-route stops producing times 30-60 min too late
Fix 3-departure display: nextDepartures padded with future departures up to 3 when fewer are catchable — ensures renderer always shows 3 departure times
Fix train alt stop IDs: build same-name station platform IDs for metro GTFS-RT matching (same pattern as trams) — enables alt-stop cascade tier for trains
Fix coord-proximity destination: prefer GTFS-RT headsign over line name for tram departure destination display

Fix inter-leg timing dependency: when all GTFS-RT departures are before user's calculated arrival at a stop, project next departure from observed headway instead of falling back to uncatchable nearest departure — prevents stale 0 MIN countdown and past DEPART times on downstream transit legs

Fix Next subtitle catchability: nextDepartures now filtered by arrivalAtStopMs before display — 'Next: X, Y, Z min LIVE' only shows departures the user can actually reach based on cumulative walk+transit+walk timing

Fix renderer cumulative timing: Location 1 subtitle calculation now uses journeyContribution (wait + transit) instead of minutes (countdown from NOW) for cumulative — prevents double-counting walk time to transit stops

Fix train line name from catchable departure: line/route name update from actual catchable departure now applies to all transit types (was tram-only) — prevents showing wrong line when multiple lines serve the same station

Fix tram GTFS-RT stop ID mismatch: new alt-stop-id cascade tier tries GTFS_STOP_NAMES aliases and nearby stops on the same road (within 350m) — bridges gap when the user's exact stop ID doesn't appear in the feed's trip data
Aggregate alt stop ID departures across all matching stops with trip ID dedup — captures full departure set when different trips visit different stops along the same road
Multi-route tram support: findMatchingDeparture skips route filter for trams (user catches whichever route arrives first), dynamic route number updated from actual catchable departure
Train direction filter in raw feed fallback: outbound trains no longer display as going to citybound destinations (e.g. Frankston incorrectly shown as 'to Parliament Station')
Coord-proximity route-only fallback: when route filter confirms correct line but no stops have coordinates, accepts trip using earliest future stop_time_update as approximation

Fix catchable departure timing: subtitle shows ALL future live departures (Next: X, Y, Z min LIVE) while timing uses first CATCHABLE departure after user arrival — prevents uncatchable departures corrupting journey timing
Fix journey contribution cascade: downstream legs now receive correct arrivalAtStopMs because timing only uses departures the user can actually catch based on cumulative leg dependencies
Fix raw feed fallback: when no catchable departure exists in GTFS-RT window, uses estimated timing instead of closest-from-NOW (which was uncatchable and corrupted arrival calculation)

Fix route number normalisation: GTFS route IDs with leading zeros (e.g. '058') now match engine route numbers ('58') — resolves tram consistently showing 'Scheduled' despite live GTFS-RT data being available
Fix catchability fallback: when live GTFS-RT data exists but no departure is catchable from the user's arrival timing, live departure times are still displayed instead of falling back to timetable
Fix timestamp clamping: valid GTFS-RT departures >180 min away no longer incorrectly set isTimetableEstimate — low-frequency services stay marked as live
Fix route matching fallback: findMatchingDeparture keeps all departures when route filter empties instead of returning null — prevents silent discard of live data

Fix tram GTFS-RT matching: coord-proximity search now accepts address coordinates as fallback when stop ID not in VIC_TRAM_STOPS_WITH_COORDS (e.g. stop 1567 stored as 18705) — enables live tram data that was previously falling to timetable

Fix train departure catchability: findMatchingDeparture now collects ALL departure times (was limited to first 5 which could all be before user arrival, causing timetable fallback on low-frequency services)

Fix City Loop filtering: shared filterCityLoopPreference() with soft-preference — when Loop services are infrequent (>20 min gap vs direct), accepts direct trains to Flinders Street instead of forcing 48-min wait for rare Loop service
Fix City Loop filter consistency: filterUnavailableTransitLegs now uses same finalStop-based filter as findMatchingDeparture (was using !isMetroTunnel proxy, causing filter/matcher disagreement)
Fix timetable subtitle stability: 'Scheduled ~Xmin' now shows transit ride duration (stable) instead of departure countdown (volatile across renders)
Fix tram GTFS-RT cascade diagnostics: cascade attempt tracking (tier, tried, found) attached to _feedInfo for diagnosing stop-ID matching failures

Fix coordinate-proximity closest-route preference: at intersections where multiple tram routes have nearby stops, only the route with the physically closest stop is matched — prevents wrong route selection

Fix coordinate-proximity departure time accuracy: find all stops within radius with future departures and pick earliest, instead of single closest stop that may have a past departure (tram already passed it)
Fix broad fallback City Loop filtering: add finalStop to processAnyRouteDepartures() departure objects — enables correct filtering of trains that do not serve City Loop stations
Fix tram GTFS-RT matching: coordinate-proximity search now runs before route-level median heuristic for trams, producing accurate departure times
Fix coordinate-proximity direction heuristic: removed flawed isApproaching position-based heuristic, use matched stop departure time directly within 500m search radius
Fix live data detection: gtfs-rt-coord source now recognised in status badge logic
Fix tram route naming consistency: route number populated from detection/engine regardless of GTFS-RT availability
Fix _feedInfo.matchMethod diagnostics: coordinate-proximity matches now tracked as coord-proximity

CCDash Renderer v3.2: per-leg element scaling prevents overlap with 7+ journey legs, coffee leg redesign (no number bubble, no time box, coffee icon replaces [OK] text, centre-aligned label), route/line names in transit leg titles (e.g. Sandringham to Parliament, Route 19 to City)
Timetable fallback now shows 3 estimated departures with headway instead of single Scheduled ~Xmin
buildLegTitle: train legs show line name, tram legs show Route N format
Fix NSW lightrail mode mapping: mode 1 (tram) now routes to lightrail feed instead of silently falling back to sydneytrains
Fix QLD mode completeness: modes 1 and 3 mapped to SEQ unified feed (prevents undefined fallback)
Ferry live data: ferryStopId detection, getDepartures(ferryStopId, 4) call, and ferry disruption fetching for NSW/QLD
State parameter propagated through route-level and all-trips fallback functions — non-VIC departures use tripHeadsign for destination
All transit modes that have GTFS-RT feeds now produce live departure data when API key is valid
TURNKEY MULTI-STATE: CommuteCompute Engine v4.1 — state-independent live data for all Australian states
NSW and QLD GTFS-RT live data wired via existing STATE_API_CONFIG endpoints (Transport for NSW, TransLink SEQ)
SA/WA/TAS/NT/ACT graceful degradation — returns empty with diagnostics instead of throwing error
Expanded fallback stop data: ~200 new stops across Sydney, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth, Hobart, Canberra, Darwin
Suburb-to-stop mappings for all 7 non-VIC capital cities (Sydney 23 suburbs, Brisbane 14, Adelaide 11, Perth 11, etc.)
processGtfsRtDepartures() guarded for non-VIC: Metro Tunnel and City Loop direction detection skipped outside Melbourne
getWeather() timezone parameterised — uses state-specific timezone instead of hardcoded Melbourne
getServiceAlerts() state-aware — fetches disruptions for all GTFS-RT-capable states, not just VIC
Generate script template (generate-state-reference.js) for future full GTFS reference data per state
CCDash Renderer v3.1: robust coffee skip detection (status + canGet), coffee time box rendering enabled, skipped coffee shows ~X MIN duration, diversion status bar with delay badge
Demo mode leg mapping: forwards delayMinutes, canGet, extraTime, departTime, divertedStop, cancelReason to renderer
Journey scenarios enriched with canGet, divertedStop fields for correct demo rendering
STATE-AWARE LIVE DATA: opendata-client.js refactored from Victoria-only to multi-state architecture supporting VIC, NSW, QLD GTFS-RT endpoints
State-aware transit API routing: fetchGtfsRt(), getDepartures(), getDisruptions() now accept state parameter and route to correct authority endpoint with correct auth headers
Graceful degradation for states without GTFS-RT (SA, WA, TAS, NT, ACT): returns empty with diagnostic _feedInfo.matchMethod='no-gtfs-rt'
Fixed missing _feedInfo on null feed return in getDepartures() — previously returned bare [] without diagnostics
Aligned VIC key validation headers with fetch headers: both now send dual Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key + KeyId
State-aware stop detection: detectStopIdsFromAddress() uses fallback-timetables.js for non-VIC coordinate-based nearest-stop lookup
State threaded through api/commutecompute.js apiOptions to all getDepartures/getDisruptions calls
WCAG 2.2 AA contrast fix: #94a3b8 replaced with #cbd5e1 across admin panel analytics (4.2:1 -> 7.5:1 ratio on dark backgrounds)
Endpoint unification docs: 16 stale /api/screen references updated to /api/commutecompute in DEVELOPMENT-RULES.md and ARCHITECTURE.md (firmware-context refs preserved)
DCO CI enforcement: dco-check job added to .gitlab-ci.yml lint stage, validates Signed-off-by on merge request commits
Trade mark status corrected to Accepted Early per IP Australia primary source letters
Format-default CI test (8 cases) protecting POST=json/GET=png boundary
15 stale /api/screen references fixed in user-facing documentation
README project scale updated (460 files, 472,000+ lines)
6 context-aware compliance scanner extensions added
Package-lock.json synced to v5.0.1
UNIFIED ENGINE: Merged api/screen.js into api/commutecompute.js — single endpoint for admin JSON, e-ink PNG/BMP, and debug diagnostics. Zero divergence between admin and device output.
GTFS coordinate-based stop detection: findNearestStops() searches 226 metro stations, 1637 tram stops, 4151 bus stops by proximity. Replaces suburb name matching as primary detection.
Engine alighting intelligence: coordinate-based destination resolution, no hardcoded Flinders Street Station fallback. Alighting stops determined by proximity to work coordinates.
Shared haversine utility (src/utils/haversine.js): single source of truth replacing 3 duplicate implementations
Shared utility extraction: time-format.js, fetch-retry.js, suburb-extract.js, config-token.js — 10 functions deduplicated across 6+ endpoint files
Renderer CCDash v3.0: transit countdown fix (trusts leg.minutes, 180-min sanity cap), cafe closed case-insensitive matching, confidence context line rendering, subtitle overflow protection
AltTransit gate fix: only activates when ALL transit is cancelled/suspended (not on any disruption or low confidence)
Walk merge name resolution: uses stopName/stationName before generic 'destination'
Fullscreen/livedash endpoint deduplication: removed unused imports, shared config-token decoder
Zone endpoint deduplication: 15 duplicate functions eliminated across 4 zone files
Backward-compatible vercel.json rewrite: /api/screen → /api/commutecompute (firmware devices continue to work)
Format detection: POST defaults to JSON (admin), GET defaults to PNG (device)
GTFS reference regenerated with coordinates: VIC_METRO_STATIONS now includes lat/lon, new VIC_TRAM_STOPS_WITH_COORDS and VIC_BUS_STOPS_WITH_COORDS exports
System v5.0.0, Engine v4.0, Renderer v3.0, Spec CCDashDesignV16.0 UNLOCKED
Suburb extraction: home/work display suburb from Places API addressComponents or Nominatim fallback
Transit-to-walk conversion: removed transit legs become estimated walks (train 4x, tram 2.5x, bus 3x speed ratios)
Consecutive walk merging handles chains of adjacent walks with last segment title
Lifestyle obligation styling: black-filled box for positive obligations only, plain text for passive notices
Admin panel edits persist to Vercel KV via POST /api/admin/preferences with geocoding
Address autocomplete uses KV-stored Google Places API key
Setup wizard extracts suburb from Places API addressComponents (locality/sublocality)
Ghost train fix: inline Metro Tunnel destination detection in filterUnavailableTransitLegs
Security hardening: auth deny-by-default, CORS restriction, KV-first config
CCDashDesignV15 spec upgraded to match all real implementation changes
System v4.2.0, Engine v3.1, Renderer v2.1, Spec CCDashDesignV15.0 UNLOCKED
CommuteCompute Engine v3.1: Metro Tunnel citybound detection via trip sequence analysis
Direction-based train filtering (any citybound train, not route-locked)
Tram route-level future-departure heuristic for accurate countdown timing
LifestyleContext v1.2: all-day hourly forecast for weather equipment decisions
CCDash Renderer v2.1: V15.0 spec-compliant box sizing, fonts, status indicators
Unified cafe closed/skipped rendering path in renderer
Inner-city stop IDs expanded for Metro Tunnel stations (Town Hall, State Library, Parkville, Arden, Anzac)
All document versions UNLOCKED for active development
CommuteCompute Engine v3.0: rewritten buildCCDashLegs() for multi-modal transit support
Shared GTFS stop names module (src/data/gtfs-stop-names.js) used by screen.js and commutecompute.js
CCDash Renderer v2.0: unified confidence labels (ON TIME/AT RISK/UNLIKELY), AM/PM fix
AltTransit now triggers on delays, cancellations, and low confidence (not just no-transit)
Admin Panel v5.0: dynamic version loading, fixed config tab, fixed Last Updated timestamp
Removed redundant /api/calculate-journey endpoint
Hardware naming standardised to TRMNL device names across 15+ files
Route alternatives endpoint fixed (undefined variable references removed)
All page footers now load versions dynamically from /api/version
Flasher page: added cc-system-footer and sanitize() function
E-ink display: suburb-level location names instead of full geocoded addresses
AltTransit guard: only activates when ALL public transit is cancelled
Admin analytics: DepartureConfidence and LifestyleContext engines integrated into /api/commutecompute
Admin panel v4.0: full redesign with sidebar navigation, branded sticky header, section cards
Admin panel CSS variables aligned with privacy/help design system
CommuteCompute Engine upgraded from v2.3 to v2.4
CCDash Renderer upgraded from v1.80 to v1.81
AGPL-3.0 dual license applied across 126+ files with SPDX headers
Comprehensive compliance audit: branding, licensing, security hardening
All frontend pages updated with cc-system-footer and Inter font
